seo.title: Self-Hosting tools directory
seo.description: A developer-first directory of Self-Hosting tools and references.

content.intro: A curated directory of infrastructure tools and services for developers looking to maintain full control over their deployment stack, optimize hosting costs, and ensure data sovereignty.

entries.name: Coolify
entries.description: An open-source, self-hosted alternative to Heroku and Netlify that manages applications, databases, and services via a web UI.
pros: Supports Docker, Nixpacks, and static sites
cons: Still in active development with occasional breaking changes
Resource intensive for very small VPS instances

entries.name: Traefik
entries.description: A modern HTTP reverse proxy and load balancer designed to deploy microservices with automatic configuration discovery.
pros: Rich middleware ecosystem for auth and headers
cons: YAML configuration syntax can be verbose
Steeper learning curve compared to Caddy

entries.name: Caddy
entries.description: A powerful, enterprise-ready open-source web server with automatic HTTPS written in Go.
cons: Plugin management requires custom builds
Documentation for advanced JSON config is complex

entries.name: Uptime Kuma
entries.description: A self-hosted monitoring tool that tracks website uptime and service health with a clean dashboard.
pros: Supports multi-protocol monitoring (HTTP, TCP, Ping, DNS)
cons: No long-term metrics storage like Prometheus
Single-user focus lacks advanced RBAC

entries.name: Portainer
entries.description: A lightweight management UI that allows you to easily manage your different Docker environments.
cons: Business features locked behind paid tier
Can become a single point of failure for container management

entries.name: Hetzner Cloud
entries.description: European-based cloud provider offering high-performance VPS instances with strict GDPR compliance.
pros: ISO 27001 certified data centers in Germany and Finland
Clean API and Terraform provider support
cons: Strict account verification process
Limited global regions compared to AWS/GCP

entries.name: Restic
entries.description: A fast, secure, and efficient backup program that supports multiple storage backends.
pros: Native support for S3, SFTP, and local storage
cons: CLI-only interface may be difficult for beginners
Pruning old backups can be resource-intensive

entries.name: Watchtower
entries.description: A process for automating Docker container base image updates.
pros: Completely automated image polling and restarts
Can notify via multiple channels when updates occur
cons: Automatic updates can break apps if semver is not followed
Requires access to the Docker socket

entries.name: Dokku
entries.description: A Docker-powered mini-Heroku that helps you build and manage the lifecycle of applications.
pros: Large plugin ecosystem for databases and SSL
cons: CLI-centric management (no official web UI)
Limited to single-node deployments

entries.name: Tailscale
entries.description: A zero-config VPN that creates a secure mesh network between your self-hosted servers and devices.
pros: Bypasses CGNAT and complex firewall rules
Based on the secure WireGuard protocol
MagicDNS for easy internal service discovery
cons: Control plane is closed source (unless using Headscale)
Adds minor latency compared to direct connections
